scope.summary: Implement blend-shape avatar import that stores multiple body-proxy states keyed by morph-target weight and exposes a BlendShapeRefitRequest API (blend_t interpolation) that warm-starts the XPBD solver from the cached prior equilibrium for interactive body-shape exploration
acceptance_criteria: BlendShapeAvatar type stores a Vec<(blend_key: String, weight: f32, body_proxy_id: String)> interpolation table in a JSONB column on tailor_avatars
BlendShapeRefitRequest (blend_t in [0,1], warm_start: bool) is accepted by POST /api/tailor/refit/blend and dispatched as a sandbox refit run

When warm_start=true and a prior equilibrium cache exists, the solver initializes particle positions from the cached state and skips the three-pass drape init, completing in <= 50% of a cold-start run time
When warm_start=true but no cache exists, the engine falls back to three-pass init and logs a warning (not an error)
Refit validation gate (REFIT_INTERSECTION_FREE, REFIT_SEAM_CLOSURE, UV_VALIDITY, MESH_TOPOLOGY) runs regardless of warm_start
cargo test blend_shape::test_warm_start_faster_than_cold and blend_shape::test_warm_start_fallback both pass
scope.risk_if_missed: MD 2026.0 Blend Shape Avatar is a key feature for MetaHuman and character-creator workflows where the operator adjusts sliders continuously. Without warm-start refit, every slider increment requires a full cold drape, making interactive body-shape exploration prohibitively slow.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
